_comment: Eval set for the using-hse-skills catalog router. FOUR cases: (1) refuse-on-vague (CONV-4), (2) triggering-precision (D-06), (3) recommendation-accuracy / multi-skill-chain (the re-roofing chain — graded against the golden output evals/output/using-hse-skills.md; the Design-B asymmetric run sheet — one Context Capsule + lean continuation prompts + the Step-4a accept/edit→re-recommend loop), (4) de_identification — the Context-Capsule + continuation-prompt de-id PAIR. Eval 4 wires the CLEAN fixture (evals/files/deid-clean.md, the capsule + lean continuation prompts) as the intake so the per-skill eval gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK negative (evals/files/deid-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (the PAIR contract: leak.auto_fail is True, clean.auto_fail is False), mirroring the canary --deid-selftest. rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate; CI runs the deterministic graders only.

evals.expected_behavior: The router does NOT recommend anything yet. It runs the structured intake one question at a time and refuses to recommend a skill or emit a run sheet until intent + the six shared facets (scope, named subject, output/success, jurisdiction, industry, exposed population) are concrete. Q-SUBJECT is the specificity anchor — a vague subject is refused.
expectations: Runs the structured intake one question at a time before any recommendation — does not guess a skill from the vague request.
Refuses to proceed on the vague subject — presses for the specific task/site/asset (Q-SUBJECT specificity anchor).
Does not recommend a skill or emit a run sheet until intent + the six shared facets are captured and echoed back for confirmation.
Does not produce a generic or template HSE artifact itself (specificity dimension).

evals.query: Write a toolbox talk for tonight's night shift on lifting heavy boxes in the warehouse.
evals.expected_behavior: This is a CLEAR single-skill request that names its own artifact. The router DEFERS — it points the user straight at the toolbox-talk skill (or invokes it) rather than running a full router elicitation. It does NOT hijack the request into a multi-skill recommendation chain.
expectations: Recognises the request already names its artifact (a toolbox talk) and DEFERS — points straight at the toolbox-talk skill (D-06, no hijack).
Does NOT run a full router elicitation or build a multi-skill chain for an unambiguous single-skill request.
Engages fully only on meta/ambiguous or multi-deliverable intent — not on a request that names its own deliverable.

evals.query: I need to make a re-roofing job safe end to end — strip and re-roof Block C, working at height, own workers plus a roofing contractor, UK construction. I need the risk assessment, the safe-work method, the permit, and a toolbox talk the crew can sign.
evals.expected_behavior: The router elicits/echoes the shared facts, reads references/skill-index.yaml, and recommends the ORDERED chain risk-assessment -> job-safety-analysis -> permit-to-work -> toolbox-talk, each with a one-line WHY tying it to the intent. Before any handover it offers the user a choice to accept the echoed context as-is, edit it, or re-scope — and an EDIT re-drives the Step-2 match so the chain refines (showing what changed) before any skill runs. It de-identifies the captured facts FIRST, then composes the Design-B ASYMMETRIC run sheet (ONE de-identified Context Capsule = the Step-0 facts + the SEQUENCE MAP emitted once, Step 1 marked run-in-place NOT a fresh-chat copy-paste block, and Steps 2+ as lean continuation prompts that REFERENCE the Context Capsule + ATTACH the named prior skill OUTPUT + give the step DELTA, no repeated full shared context). Per Design D (persist-then-clear) the router PERSISTS that run sheet as a durable markdown artifact — a file on a file-capable host (path surfaced) or a clearly-delimited copy-paste block on a chat-only host — and PRESENTS it BEFORE Step 1 (file-first ordering); the Step-1 skill is invoked ONLY on the user's explicit clearance ('go' / 'proceed' / 'run step 1') and does NOT auto-fire after confirm-or-refine (an 'edit' routes back to the confirm step and re-persists). Role labels only; no leaked identifier.
expectations: Recommends the ordered chain risk-assessment -> job-safety-analysis -> permit-to-work -> toolbox-talk (correct skills, correct dependency order).
Each recommended skill carries a one-line WHY tied to the elicited intent/success-criteria (ROUTE-03).
Emits ONE de-identified Context Capsule (the Step-0 facts + the SEQUENCE MAP) once at the top of the run sheet — not a per-step full-context repeat.

Step 1 is marked run-in-place and is NOT part of the copy-paste-to-fresh-chat set; Steps 2+ are lean continuation prompts that REFERENCE the Context Capsule + ATTACH the named prior skill OUTPUT + give the step DELTA (WHY / RUN / REFERENCE / ATTACH / DELTA / DEPENDENCY / FEEDS).

Offers the user an accept / edit / re-scope choice on the echoed context, and an EDIT re-drives the Step-2 match to refine the chain (showing what changed) before any skill runs — the recommendation-accuracy loop (R2).

The run sheet is PERSISTED as a durable markdown artifact — a file (path surfaced, e.g. hse-run-sheet-<slug>.md) on a file-capable host, or a clearly-delimited copy-paste save-block on a chat-only host — and the saved file is the complete standalone plan (Context Capsule + ordered chain + Step-1 record + all Steps-2+ continuation prompts) (R1).
The persisted run sheet is PRESENTED BEFORE Step 1 (file-first ordering) — the emit+persist+present step precedes the Step-1-in-place step (R2).

The Step-1 skill is invoked ONLY on the user's explicit clearance ('go' / 'proceed' / 'run step 1') and does NOT auto-fire after confirm-or-refine; an 'edit' routes back to the confirm step and re-persists; the clearance gate applies on every host (R3).
The captured facts are de-identified FIRST — role labels only ([SITE-1], [ROLE: site manager], [CONTRACTOR]); no leaked name, address, phone, ID, or health detail.

evals.query: A manager pastes a raw handoff note for the re-roofing job that contains the site lead's real name, home address, phone, national ID, and a health condition, and asks for the run sheet to chain the recommended skills.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before the run sheet is written. The Context Capsule, every continuation prompt, AND the PERSISTED run-sheet file (the durable de-identified .md written on a file-capable host, or the copy-paste save-block presented on a chat-only host) are all de-id surfaces. The real name, home address, phone, national ID, and health detail are detected and pseudonymized to role labels; any <5 injury cell is suppressed/aggregated; no re-identification key is embedded in the capsule, any continuation prompt, or the persisted run-sheet file (and the filename is a role-label slug, never a verbatim name/site). Any verbatim identifier, address, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the circulated capsule, continuation prompts, or the persisted run-sheet file is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture is wired in the PAIR so the keyless deterministic de-id grader scans the leaked text and MUST catch it; the paired clean fixture (files/deid-clean.md, which mirrors the persisted run-sheet shape — capsule + lean continuation prompts) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified persisted run-sheet file.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before the run sheet is written — the identifiers (name, address, phone, national ID, health detail) are detected and listed up front.

The real name is replaced with a stable role label; the home address, phone, national ID, and health detail are never carried verbatim into the Context Capsule, any continuation prompt, or the persisted run-sheet file; any <5 injury cell is suppressed/aggregated.

No real name, address, phone, ID, or health detail in the circulated Context Capsule, continuation prompts, or the persisted run-sheet file, and no re-identification key / name-to-label mapping embedded (and the persist filename is a role-label slug, not a verbatim name/site) — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (the gate is live); the paired clean fixture (files/deid-clean.md, which mirrors the persisted run-sheet file shape — capsule + lean continuation prompts) does NOT trip (no false-positive).
